questions.question: Who was Ramabai Ranade?
questions.answer: She was a social reformer who promoted women’s education in India.

questions.question: Name one school founded by women reformers.
questions.answer: SNDT Women’s University was founded by Dhondo Keshav Karve.

questions.question: Who was Rokeya Sakhawat Hossain?
questions.answer: She was a reformer who worked for Muslim girls’ education in Bengal.

questions.question: Which reformer wrote her autobiography to inspire literacy?
questions.answer: Rashsundari Devi.

questions.question: Name one social issue addressed by women reformers.
questions.answer: Child marriage.

questions.question: Who promoted widow remarriage in 19th-century India?
questions.answer: Ramabai Ranade.

questions.question: Which movement encouraged girls’ literacy in Bengal?
questions.answer: Rokeya Sakhawat Hossain’s movement for Muslim girls’ education.

questions.question: Name one book written by women reformers in the 19th century.
questions.answer: Aamar Jiban by Rashsundari Devi.

questions.question: What was the main aim of women reformers’ schools?
questions.answer: To provide education and literacy to girls.

questions.question: Who was the first principal of SNDT Women’s University?
questions.answer: Dhondo Keshav Karve.

questions.question: Name one method used by reformers to promote education.
questions.answer: Setting up schools and literacy classes.

questions.question: Which reformer helped reduce stigma against widows?
questions.answer: Ramabai Ranade.

questions.question: What was the impact of women’s literacy on society?
questions.answer: It empowered women to participate in social reform and express their views.

questions.question: Name one reformer who worked for women’s education in Maharashtra.
questions.answer: Ramabai Ranade.

questions.question: Which social practice was challenged by early women reformers?
questions.answer: Child marriage and restrictions on widow remarriage.

questions.question: Who wrote Aamar Jiban?
questions.answer: Rashsundari Devi.

questions.question: Which reformer focused on education for Muslim girls?
questions.answer: Rokeya Sakhawat Hossain.

questions.question: Name one tool used by reformers to spread awareness.
questions.answer: Pamphlets and books.

questions.question: Who inspired women to take leadership roles in education?
questions.answer: Ramabai Ranade and Rokeya Sakhawat Hossain.

questions.question: Which reformer emphasized self-expression through writing?
questions.answer: Rashsundari Devi.

questions.question: What was the role of women reformers in rural society?
questions.answer: They established schools, literacy programs, and encouraged women’s participation in social activities.

questions.question: Name one barrier faced by women reformers.
questions.answer: Patriarchal resistance from families and society.

questions.question: Which reformer worked in Bengal for women’s literacy?
questions.answer: Rokeya Sakhawat Hossain.

questions.question: Why was women’s education important for social reform?
questions.answer: It empowered women to challenge social norms and participate in reform activities.

questions.question: Name one legacy of 19th-century women reformers.
questions.answer: Foundation for women’s education and legal rights in modern India.
